rag_rules: Assumptions and guarantees for component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should only include assumptions and guarantees that reference visible feature names or named constants.
Subcomponent ports should not be used in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
When defining bounds, use visible feature names and case-provided thresholds to ensure contract validity.
Preserve AADL architecture by placing annex content in appropriate scopes without altering component structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use valid AADL scope placement for AGREE annex blocks: component types for port-level contracts, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
Ensure that all feature references in contracts are visible within the target scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific assumptions or guarantees in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
